name: Vega de Liordes Trail
name_original: Vega de Liordes desde Fuente Dé – Parque Nacional de Picos de Europa (PR-PNPE 25)
description: This difficult 6.8-mile (10.9 km) jogging route in Picos de Europa National Park offers strenuous climbs and stunning alpine scenery.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Camaleño are set within the Picos de Europa National Park, offering a diverse mountainous landscape for runners. The region features towering peaks, lush valleys, and deep gorges, with terrain ranging from well-trodden paths to challenging mountain trails. Runners can explore areas characterized by limestone formations, verdant forests, and winding rivers, providing varied elevation changes. Best jogging routes around Camaleño The most popular jogging route is Brez - Canal de las Arredondas Trail, a 3.4 miles (5.5 km) trail that takes about 44 minutes to complete. This route features an ascent of over 230 meters, offering views of the surrounding mountainous terrain. Another top favourite among local runners is Mirador del Cable-Bulnes, Refugio Cabaña Verónica, Torre Blanca — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa, a difficult 8.2 miles (13.3 km) path. This trail provides access to high-altitude areas and panoramic vistas within the national park. Local runners also love the Vega de Liordes Trail, a 6.8 miles (10.9 km) trail leading through valleys and high mountain terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 27 minutes. Jogging around Camaleño is defined by dramatic mountainous terrain, lush valleys, and access to the Picos de Europa National Park. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Camaleño are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 6 reviews. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Camaleño's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Camaleño?
A: Komoot offers a selection of over 20 running routes around Camaleño. These trails cater to various preferences, from moderate paths through valleys to challenging mountain runs within the Picos de Europa National Park.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for jogging trails in Camaleño?
A: The running trails in Camaleño are predominantly challenging, with 18 routes rated as difficult and 5 as moderate. There are currently no easy routes listed, indicating that most trails involve significant elevation changes and varied terrain suitable for experienced runners.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running options in the Camaleño area?
A: While many routes are challenging, the region does offer some options suitable for families. The "Bajo los Picos Trail," starting in Mogrovejo, is described as an easy-to-moderate circular route of around 9 km, featuring beautiful pastures and villages with impressive views. Always check route details for specific suitability.

Q: What kind of terrain and elevation can I expect on Camaleño's running trails?
A: Camaleño's running routes are characterized by dramatic mountainous terrain, lush valleys, and deep gorges within the Picos de Europa National Park. You can expect varied surfaces, from unpaved tracks through deciduous woodlands to rough, steep, and rocky sections in higher elevations, with significant elevation gains and descents.

Q: Can I find circular running routes around Camaleño?
A: Yes, there are circular running routes available. For example, the "Bajo los Picos Trail" from Mogrovejo is a circular route. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1228828627/circular-from-fuente-de-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular from Fuente Dé — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a challenging 4.6 km loop with substantial elevation changes.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while running in Camaleño?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and landmarks. You can encounter towering peaks, lush valleys like Tornos de Liordes, and the dramatic Hermida Gorge. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/280873" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Cable Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, accessible via the Fuente Dé cable car, and the source of the Deva River. The area also features ancient beech forests and glacial cirques.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer panoramic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. Many routes provide stunning vistas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229651912/mirador-del-cable-bulnes-refugio-cabana-veronica-torre-blanca-parque-nacional-picos-de-europa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Cable-Bulnes, Refugio Cabaña Verónica, Torre Blanca — Parque Nacional Picos de Europa&lt;/a&gt; trail, for instance, offers access to high-altitude areas and incredible panoramic views. Other viewpoints like Mirador de San Miguel are also worth incorporating into your run.

Q: What do other runners say about the trails in Camaleño?
A: The running routes in Camaleño are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 6 reviews. Runners often praise the dramatic mountainous terrain, the diverse landscapes within the Picos de Europa National Park, and the variety of trails available for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any specific routes that lead through valleys or forests?
A: Yes, you can find routes that traverse picturesque valleys and forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999019540/vega-de-liordes-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vega de Liordes Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through valleys and high mountain terrain. Areas like the Hayedo de Edes (beech forest) also provide unique natural settings for running.

Q: Can I combine running with sightseeing in Camaleño?
A: Definitely. Many routes allow you to combine your run with cultural or scenic stops. The surroundings of the Monastery of Santo Toribio de Liébana offer breathtaking scenery. Villages like Mogrovejo and Brez also serve as scenic starting points for routes, allowing you to explore charming towns while enjoying your run.

Q: Are there any routes that start near Fuente Dé?
A: Yes, Fuente Dé is a popular starting point for many trails. You can take the cable car up to access high-altitude routes, or start directly from the base. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1229403307/espinama-and-fuente-de-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Espinama and Fuente Dé Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 17 km route that starts and ends near Fuente Dé, offering significant elevation changes and stunning views.

Q: Is it possible to run in Camaleño during winter?
A: Winter running in Camaleño, especially within the Picos de Europa National Park, can be challenging due to snow and ice at higher elevations. While lower valley routes might be accessible, it's crucial to check weather conditions, trail status, and potential avalanche warnings before heading out. Appropriate gear for cold and potentially snowy conditions is essential.
tips: La Vega de Liordes is an impressive meadow located in the upper part of the Central Massif of the Picos de Europa, in the Leonese municipality of Posada de Valdeón. This place is known for its natural beauty and its spectacular mountainous surroundings.
An impressive and wonderful place! A clear example of the beauty of the Picos de Europa National Park, a place that cannot be missing from your list of places to visit!

This place is famous for its impressive glacial cirque and the Fuente Dé Cable Car, which allows visitors to ascend to a height of 1,823 metres in just 4 minutes. 
Fuente Dé is also a starting point for various hiking and mountaineering routes, such as the ascent to Alto de la Triguera and the circuit around Peña Remoña. In addition, the area is rich in biodiversity and offers opportunities for observing flora and fauna.

faqs.question: Is the Vega de Liordes Trail suitable for jogging or running?
faqs.answer: While komoot categorizes this route for jogging, the Vega de Liordes Trail is primarily a challenging hiking route due to its demanding terrain and significant elevation changes. It's best suited for experienced trail runners seeking a strenuous workout rather than casual jogging. Expect sections with goat paths, loose debris, and steep ascents and descents.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is 100% unpaved and features varied, demanding surfaces. You'll encounter goat paths, debris couloirs like the Canal de Pedabejo, and sections of light gray limestone with many dolines. The Los Tornos path, an old mining track, involves a long, steep descent with many zigzags and loose debris. The Vega de Liordes itself is an open meadow with grasslands and wetlands, which might offer some smoother, albeit potentially damp, sections.

faqs.question: How difficult is this trail?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'difficult' by komoot and generally considered 'Heavy' or 'Hard' by hikers. It requires very good fitness, sure-footedness, sturdy shoes, and alpine experience. The full route involves significant height gain and can take 5.5 to 6.5 hours to complete. Special caution is advised in case of snow or fog.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail typically starts from Fuente Dé. There is parking available at Fuente Dé, which is also the location of the cable car. Taking the cable car can help bypass the initial abrupt ascent if you prefer.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery and natural features can I expect?
faqs.answer: You'll experience spectacular and diverse scenery, transitioning from forests of oaks and beeches near Fuente Dé to alpine pastures. The Vegas de Liordes is an idyllic glaciokarstic depression with exuberant mountain flora. Expect impressive views of towering limestone formations and peaks like Torre de Lambrion, Peña Remoña, Pico San Carlos, and Pico de la Padiorna, as well as the Cirque of the Picos de Europa.

faqs.question: What wildlife might I encounter on the trail?
faqs.answer: The Picos de Europa National Park is rich in wildlife. You might spot chamois (rebecos), deer, and wild boars. Brown bears and wolves also inhabit the area, though sightings are less common.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit Vega de Liordes?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit is typically during the warmer months, from late spring to early autumn, when the weather is more stable and snow has melted from higher elevations. Avoid winter unless you have extensive alpine experience and equipment, as conditions can be very challenging with snow and ice.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Dogs are generally allowed in the Picos de Europa National Park, but they must be kept on a leash to protect wildlife and livestock. Always check the latest park regulations before your visit, especially regarding specific areas or times of year.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Picos de Europa National Park?
faqs.answer: There is no general entrance fee to access the Picos de Europa National Park. However, certain activities or access to specific areas might require permits or have restrictions. It's always advisable to check the official park website for any current regulations, especially if planning to camp or access sensitive ecological zones.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily following the Vega de Liordes trail (100%), the route also passes through sections of the Transcantábrica, Puertos de Áliva - Fuente De, and Senda del Mercadillo trails.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this challenging route?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's difficulty and terrain, you should bring sturdy hiking boots with good ankle support, appropriate layers of clothing for changing mountain weather, plenty of water, high-energy snacks, a map and compass or GPS device, and a first-aid kit. Trekking poles are highly recommended for stability on steep and loose sections.

waypoints.name: Cirque of the Picos de Europa
